Name: efficient_llm_inference
Title: Optimizing Large Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs via Hybrid CPU-GPU Attention Mechanisms
Short Hypothesis: By combining the strengths of CPU and GPU architectures, a hybrid attention mechanism can be designed to accelerate large language model inference on commodity GPUs while maintaining accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as HGCA and PLAIN have explored the use of hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms and processing-in-memory architectures to accelerate LLM inference. However, these approaches often rely on custom hardware or complex software frameworks. In contrast, our proposal focuses on developing a hybrid attention mechanism that can be implemented on commodity GPUs, leveraging the strengths of both CPU and GPU architectures to achieve efficient inference.
Abstract: Large language models have achieved state-of-the-art results in various natural language processing tasks, but their computational requirements pose significant challenges for deployment on commodity hardware. To address this, we propose a hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanism that combines the strengths of both architectures to accelerate LLM inference on commodity GPUs. Our approach enables scalable, high-throughput inference with near-full attention quality, while minimizing PCIe transfer overhead and reducing computation. We evaluate our implementation on diverse models and workloads, demonstrating superior scalability, longer sequence support, and improved performance compared to existing sparse attention baselines.
Experiments: Implement the hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanism on a commodity GPU using CUDA and Triton
Evaluate the performance of the proposed mechanism on various LLM benchmarks, including LLaMA and Qwen
Compare the results with existing sparse attention baselines, such as HGCA and PLAIN
Measure the wall-clock latency, throughput, and energy consumption of the proposed mechanism
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed mechanism may require significant modifications to existing LLM frameworks
The use of CPU-GPU attention mechanisms may introduce additional overhead due to data transfer between devices
The evaluation of the proposed mechanism may be limited by the availability of commodity GPU hardware
